description: Fig. 1. Structure characterization of the as-prepared SC-8 sample. (A) Typical high-resolution TEM image showing nanosized grains outlined by white dashed lines. Inset is a corresponding selected area electron diffraction (SAED) pattern. (B) High-resolution high-angle annular dark-field (HAADF)-scanning transmission electron microscope (STEM) image of an aggregate in the manifold structure (fig. S3). Orange solid lines represent coherent twin boundaries (CTBs) and orange dashed lines are {111} planes. (C and D) Elemental mappings of Al and Mg. (E and F) High-resolution TEM images of a grain with a CTB (E) and a twin-free grain (F) with a beam direction along the [110] zone axis. Missing atoms at several corners are circled.

Fig. 2. Structure evolution upon annealing. (A) Typical bright-field TEM image of the as-prepared NG-50 sample with a SAED pattern (inset). (B) HAADF-STEM image of the NG-50 sample annealed at $423\mathrm{K}$ for 1 hour showing nanosized precipitates at GBs outlined by white dashed lines. (C) Bright-field TEM image of precipitates in the NG-50 annealed at $483\mathrm{K}$ for 6 hours. Inset is an elemental mapping of $\mathrm{Mg}$ of a precipitate. (D) Corresponding SAED pattern from (C) indexed with $\mathrm{Al}_{3}\mathrm{Mg}_{2}$ ( $\beta$ ) and $\alpha$ -Al. (E) XRD patterns of the as-prepared and the annealed samples as indicated. The inset depicts magnified patterns of the two annealed samples. (F and G) Typical bright-field TEM image with a SAED pattern of the SC-8 sample annealed at $483\mathrm{K}$ for 6 hours.

Fig. 4. Elemental distributions upon annealing. (A to D) Typical bright-field TEM images and corresponding EDS elemental mappings of Mg in the SC-8 samples annealed at $760\mathrm{K}$ [(A) and (B)] and $723\mathrm{K}$ [(C) and (D)] for 1 hour, respectively. (E) EDS line scans of Mg concentration in the as-prepared and the annealed samples at various temperatures (as indicated) for 1 hour. (F) SEM image of the annealed NG-50 sample and EDS elemental mapping (inset) of the $\mathrm{Al}_{3}\mathrm{Mg}_{2}$ phase solidified from the melted region (arrow).
